_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.

clinical_thresholds.note: Hemorrhoidectomy is indicated for Grade III/IV hemorrhoids.

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes include detailed history, signs & symptoms, and indication for procedure?
Per rectal examination and proctoscopy report confirms hemorrhoid with or without bleeding points?

cpd_claim.q: Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and line of treatment available?

common_queries: Indication for surgery not clearly mentioned in clinical notes.
Proctoscopy report not submitted or does not confirm diagnosis of hemorrhoids.
Clinical notes lacking detailed history, signs, or symptoms.
Intra-operative clinical photographs missing or unclear.
Discharge summary missing follow-up advice or not detailed.

clinical_key_pointers: Hemorrhoids are dilated veins occurring in relation to the anus (commonly known as piles).
Hemorrhoids develop due to: Downward displacement or prolapse of the anal cushions
Loss of anchoring connective tissue in the anal cushions
Abnormal dilatation of veins in the internal hemorrhoidal venous plexus
Carcinoma of rectum – compression / thrombosis of superficial rectal veins
